steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how Hammond and Porter contributed to Bell's research. The notes show they confirmed their ancestors carried youlk when traveling — a concrete detail that supported Bell's hypothesis about the plant's distribution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Bell found that Hammond and Porter had information relevant to her study of *Platysace deflexa*'s distribution.": identifies who Hammond and Porter are without explaining what specific information they provided. ✗
  • "Hammond and Porter confirmed that DNA sequences from the *Platysace deflexa* plants Bell had gathered were in fact very similar.": credits Hammond and Porter with conclusions that belong to Bell herself. ✗
  • "Bell consulted Hammond and Porter, members of the Noongar Aboriginal group, who assisted with her study of *Platysace deflexa*, a small shrub with edible tubers.": tells us about their background rather than what they contributed to the study. ✗
  • "By indicating that their ancestors transported *Platysace deflexa*, Hammond and Porter provided information that supported Bell's hypothesis.": states the specific information they gave (ancestors carried youlk) and explains why it mattered to Bell's research. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
